symbols: Christ ascending with arms raised — the triumphant return to the Father after completing the work of redemption
red mantle — the blood of the Passion now glorified, royalty and victory
angels playing instruments — the heavenly court celebrating the King's return
cherubim supporting Christ — the throne-bearers of God carrying the Son back to glory
Mary at center below — the Church gazing heavenward, awaiting the promised return
apostles looking up — the witnesses commissioned to continue Christ's work until he comes again
description: A two-tiered vertical composition. In the upper half, a muscular figure in a flowing red mantle rises into the sky, arms raised, supported by numerous putti and cherubim on clouds. Angels with musical instruments — harps, lutes, and horns — surround him. In the lower half, a group of about fourteen figures stands on the ground looking upward. A woman in a dark blue-green robe stands at center with hands clasped in prayer. The other figures wear colorful robes in red, green, gold, and orange. The landscape behind shows green hills under a blue sky.
theological_description: Perugino depicts the Ascension as recorded by Luke: 'He was taken up before their very eyes, and a cloud hid him from their sight' (Acts 1:9). The risen Christ, bearing the marks of his Passion but now clothed in glory, returns to the Father's right hand (Mark 16:19). The musical angels echo the psalmist: 'God has ascended amid shouts of joy, the Lord amid the sounding of trumpets' (Psalm 47:5). The apostles below, gazing upward with Mary, embody the pivotal moment between Christ's physical presence and the age of the Church. Two realities are established here: Christ's present reign at God's right hand (Ephesians 1:20-21) and his promised return 'in the same way you have seen him go into heaven' (Acts 1:11). Mary's prayerful stance at the center of the apostolic community prefigures the gathering in the Upper Room where they awaited the Holy Spirit at Pentecost (Acts 1:14).

scripture_refs.reason: The Ascension narrative — Christ taken up while the apostles watch, and the angel's promise of return
The Lord Jesus was taken up into heaven and sat at the right hand of God
God ascends amid shouts of joy and trumpet sound — the musical angels enact this
God seated Christ at his right hand, far above all rule and authority
Mary and the apostles gathered in prayer after the Ascension, awaiting the Spirit
